summaries.182756: Root cause identified: Flutter's hidden element for clipboard support is only populated when the user right-clicks, because that code path is gated on a contextmenu event in `_platform_selectable_region_context_menu_web.dart`; `execCommand("copy")` and Edit-menu copy fire no such event. Comments propose listening to the browser's native `copy` event to update the hidden div for any copy trigger, which a contributor confirmed works in jsfiddle and that `execCommand` also fires the `copy` event. Renzo-Olivares agreed the `copy`-event approach is reasonable and invited a contributor to implement it, but the contributor got stuck on running web tests on Windows and understanding the engine-side hidden-input code. Status: no PR opened; waiting on a volunteer or team member to implement the fix.
summaries.66106: Root cause: calling `FocusScope.of(context).requestFocus(FocusNode())` with an unattached FocusNode closes the input connection; when `TextInputAction.next` fires it tries to traverse focus, hits the detached node, and throws `'context != null'` from `ReadingOrderTraversalPolicy`. Logs show `IInputConnectionWrapper: beginBatchEdit on inactive InputConnection`. Affects Android (confirmed on stable 3.3 and master 3.6). Status: open, no fix or workaround offered.
summaries.39900: Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.3 and master 3.7 on iOS; Android works without issues. Root cause is an iOS-specific quirk: programmatic `TextInput.hide` disconnects the text input connection so physical/HID keyboard input is no longer received, while manually hiding the inputAccessory via its own arrow button does not disconnect it. One community workaround shared involves calling `TextInput.hide` inside a controller listener on every change rather than once on focus. The issue is labeled `workaround available` but no Flutter fix has been landed.
summaries.101288: Confirmed reproducible on iOS web (Safari) on stable and master; Android web works fine. A later commenter on stable 3.19 and master 3.22 reports the large undefined blank area is no longer visible on their devices but a Scaffold background flash still occurs on keyboard dismiss on iOS Safari. No fix landed; status open with updated label noting device-specific behavior.
summaries.146057: Comments clarify the behavior: a normal `Navigator.push` inside a `CupertinoTabScaffold` nests the pushed route inside the tab scaffold, so the scaffold's `resizeToAvoidBottomInset` resizes everything including the background container. Using `rootNavigator: true` avoids the resize because it pushes above the tab scaffold. One commenter provides a workaround using `resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false` plus `EdgeInsets.only(bottom: MediaQuery.viewInsets.bottom)` padding on the container. The team member who triaged considers it expected behavior given the navigation architecture.
summaries.95194: Comments clarify the bug: when text is added programmatically via `TextEditingController` while the field is focused, the field does not scroll to keep the cursor visible if the cursor moves beyond the visible area. Reproduced on both iOS and Android on stable 2.8/master. Android partially works. The suggested workaround of re-setting `controller.selection` does not help because the underlying issue is that `EditableText` does not call `bringIntoView` after a programmatic text change. Labeled as duplicate of #11416 (closed) but reporter considers it distinct since field remains focused.
summaries.116889: Root cause explained in comments: iOS UIKit has no public API to trigger the save-password prompt programmatically; it fires automatically when the view is removed from the hierarchy, which is why the prompt appears on navigation away rather than on `finishAutofillContext()` call. Confirmed still broken on stable through 3.27.4; one commenter cannot reproduce on iOS 26.2 but still fails on iOS 18.6. `AutofillContextAction.cancel` is confirmed non-functional — it still triggers the save prompt on dispose. Team note: developers must call `finishAutofillContext(save: false)` themselves when leaving the screen without a successful login.
summaries.23198: Confirmed reproduced on iOS (stable 3.7 and master 3.9); not reproducible on Android emulator in 2023 test. One commenter in 2024 claims the issue is fixed in Flutter SDK 3.24.5 and newer, but this is unverified by the team. Status: open, no official fix confirmed.
summaries.27555: Comments provide user workarounds: `alignLabelWithHint: true` in `InputDecoration` and/or `isDense: true` + custom `contentPadding`. Confirmed still reproducible on stable 3.3 and master 3.7. No fix proposed or landed.
summaries.96520: Root cause: when a Flutter fragment does not occupy the full screen height (e.g., native bottom navigation bar present), `Scaffold` adds bottom inset padding equal to the distance from the bottom of the fragment to the bottom of the screen, causing extra whitespace above the keyboard. Affects Android (confirmed on stable 2.8 and master) and also iOS. The extra padding is confirmed via Flutter Inspector to come from Flutter's Material scaffold, not the host Android app; Cupertino does not exhibit the behavior. No fix has been landed; using `resizeToAvoidBottomInset: false` and manually handling `MediaQuery.viewInsets.bottom` is a partial workaround.
summaries.142820: Root cause explained: Android software keyboards for numeric/phone types send simulated hardware key events (unlike text-type keyboards), so `HardwareKeyboard` listeners fire and — if they return `true` — consume the input before it reaches the text field. This is keyboard-app-dependent behavior not under Flutter's control, consistently reproduced with Gboard and AOSP on Android 9–13. Team says documenting the inconsistency is the appropriate response; no framework fix planned.
summaries.90207: Root cause: on Android 12+, Virtual Display-based platform views use a `DisplayId` that doesn't match the `InputMethodManager`'s display, causing keyboard failures. Switching to Hybrid Composition (e.g., `SurfaceAndroidWebView` in webview_flutter, or `useHybridComposition: true` in flutter_inappwebview) resolves it. For `webview_flutter` >= 3.0.0, hybrid composition is the default. Issue is Android 12-specific and engine-level; no framework fix landed.
summaries.135354: Root cause identified by commenter: `TextEditingController` listeners fire before layout, so the underlying `TextPainter` still holds the old text and `getLocalRectForCaret` returns stale coordinates from the previous layout pass. Reproduced on Android (incorrect position) and iOS/web/macOS (different incorrect behavior). Workarounds: (1) use `WidgetsBinding.instance.addPostFrameCallback` to read caret position after layout, or (2) force-update `renderEditable.text` with `buildTextSpan()` before calling `getLocalRectForCaret`. Status: open, no fix proposed.
summaries.107432: Root cause identified: `WidgetSpan` is treated as a single character by the engine text APIs, but the `TextEditingController` stores the multi-character source string, causing a position mismatch. The framework PR #80185 attempted a 'replacements' API but never landed; #83537 also touched this area. Workaround confirmed: insert zero-width-space (`​`) placeholder `TextSpan`s for each additional character the widget replaces so positions align. Team states this is not on the near-term roadmap.
summaries.132638: Comments confirm iOS text-replacement autofill fails intermittently: it works on initial app install/run but stops after some usage, and reverting to Flutter 3.7.10 fixes it. Reproduced on stable 3.10.6; master 3.14 shows intermittent behavior (fails after running stable, works on fresh install). The issue only occurs on iOS; Android text replacement works differently (requires tapping a suggestion bar item). Status: open, team could not find a consistent repro trigger.
summaries.77512: Comments develop a detailed proposal: add `acceptTabs` and `indentSpaceCount`/`tabWidth` properties to `TextField`/`TextStyle`, and provide `IndentLineFormatter`/`ReplaceTabFormatter` input formatters. A contributor shared working workaround code using `Actions`/`Shortcuts` to intercept Tab and insert spaces or `	`. A design doc was created (linked in comments). `@justinmc` committed to folding the feature into an ongoing text editing refactor, but no PR has landed and the issue remains open.
summaries.141955: Comments confirm the bug: when a `TextField` has pre-existing text and the iOS 'From Mail' / OTP QuickType autofill suggestion is tapped, the `TextEditingController` receives no notification and the field is not updated. Native iOS apps handle this correctly. Reproduced on stable 3.16 through 3.19 on iOS 14–17. The issue was assigned to `@LongCatIsLooong` but subsequently unassigned due to inactivity. Status: closed by bot for insufficient repro info, but community confirmed the bug is reproducible with the provided sample.
